reason: Both answers cover the same core moats (workflow depth/switching costs, proprietary data feedback loops, distribution/trust, domain-specific evals, cost/margin engineering) and both correctly conclude model quality was never a durable moat, closing with an actionable reframe. No factual errors in either. A's closing statement is slightly sharper on the point that model quality was never durable, while B more explicitly names speed of iteration/shipping as itself an advantage - each nails a key point the other states more implicitly. Length and structure are comparable, neither pads unnecessarily. Overall quality is essentially even.
